creator: uqs@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hmmm, I'm not sure that applies to my system, tbh. You write "the USB-C port is TB", but I have 2 of those on the left side (T490).

The one in the back is where a pure power supply is connected. The one in the front sometimes (but not often, not necessarily when the problem happens) has a USB-C to HDMI adapter hooked up.

The only plugging and unplugging happens on the USB-A port on the right side of the laptop, as a USB switch is connected there, which I use to switch keyboard/mouse over.

Due to other bugs I no longer suspend/resume that laptop, and, well, I think the problem also happens when the HDMI adapter is not plugged in. I'll keep an eye out, just in case though.
